content.text:
Module:
Quality of Service in Networks  |
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEM2
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Learn the basic elements of QoS, the relationship between QoS and the network
Understand basic queuing management models applied to networks
Learn QoS policy models and mechanisms used to guarantee QoS in communication networks
Discover QoS implemented in next-generation networks
Recommended Prerequisites
Different types of networks and their protocols
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to QoS in IP Networks
QoS criteria (Throughput, Jitter, packet loss rate...)
Types of QoS
Application taxonomy
Multimedia and QoS (Streaming, Multicast...)
Traffic Management
Scheduling
Traffic shaping
Policing
Congestion avoidance
QoS Policies
DiffServ
IntServ
RSVP
MPLS
QoS in networks
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
Wendell Odom, Michael J. Cavanaugh, Cisco QOS Exam Certification Guide (IP Telephony Self-Study), Cisco Systems, 2005.
Jean François Susbielle, Internet, Multimedia et temps réel, Eyrolles, 2000.
Jean Louis Melin, Qualité de service sur IP, Eyrolles, 2001.
R. Steinmetz, Multimedia: Computing, Communications and Applications, Prentice Hall, 1995.
